<findings>

[observation] [warning] 9 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
[suggestion] [info] Zone "store" has files across 8 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory

</findings>

<insights>

- 9 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- Cohesion of 0.6 is the highest in this batch — the adapter registry, contracts, integration schemas, and sync CLI are tightly unified around a single storage abstraction, making this the healthiest structural unit.
- Integration schemas for Jira and Notion follow a clean extensible pattern indexed via integration-schemas/index.ts — adding new integrations requires only a new schema file and registration entry.
- src/cli/commands/sync.ts living in this zone rather than the CLI zone creates a minor structural asymmetry; if further CLI commands accumulate here the zone's purpose will blur.
- Strong cohesion (0.6) and moderate coupling (0.4) make this the best-balanced zone in the batch — the adapter-registry pattern is working well and is worth replicating elsewhere.
- src/cli/commands/sync.ts is co-located with store internals rather than the CLI zone; relocating it to the CLI layer would better reflect the architectural boundary.
- Zone "prd-storage-adapters" has files across 8 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- store → unit-analyze: 22 imports means the storage adapter layer has an upward dependency on the analysis layer — a clear architectural inversion. A foundation-layer concern (persistence) must not depend on a higher-layer concern (analysis/domain).
- store → unit-analyze: 22 imports is an architectural inversion: the storage layer (expected to be a low-level foundation) imports 22 times from the analysis zone (a higher-level domain concern). This is the most structurally dangerous inversion in the codebase because it ties persistence to domain logic, preventing either from evolving independently.
- prd-storage-adapters is cleanly isolated from fix-command and task-selection (zero cross-imports in either direction) — storage concerns are correctly invisible to command-leaf zones, which is a healthy boundary.
- src/cli/commands/sync.ts is misplaced inside the storage zone. CLI command files belong in the CLI layer; co-locating commands with their backing domain sets a precedent that would scatter the CLI layer across every domain zone if followed consistently.
- prd-storage-adapters is the only zone achieving cohesion ≥ 0.6 with coupling ≤ 0.4, yet no zone in the codebase achieves both high cohesion (>0.6) AND low coupling (<0.3) simultaneously. The absence of a true foundation/types layer means every zone must import peer zones for shared definitions, keeping all coupling floors elevated. Extracting src/schema/index.ts and shared PRD types into a dedicated src/foundation/ module would give every zone a downward-only dependency sink and is the single change most likely to reduce the coupling floor across all zones simultaneously.
- Zone "store" has files across 8 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- [call graph] 231 internal calls, 48 outgoing, 57 incoming (cohesion: 0.83, coupling: 0.17)

</insights>
